column3: THIS IS A FAMILY ([ICON:FAMILY]) YO.
Having a few [ARCHER:S] in the early-game can really save your bacon.
[Turns|TURN] are pretty gosh-darn important in this game.
Testing our [COLOR:GOOD-GREEN]fancy-ish
new [ICON:FAMILY] family system.[/COLOR]
The question now is whether EVERYTHING works, as it's not entirely likely. When you're programming stuff you KNOW that SOMETHING is gonna be broken. Just the way life goes. :(

column3: Deep Water is a type of [Terrain|TERRAIN] which essentially represents the open ocean, and was not traversable during this time period.
Shallow Water is a type of [Terrain|TERRAIN] that can be passed through by [GALLEY:S], and may contain valuable [sources|DEPOSIT] of [FISH] that can be harvested by a [FISHERMAN].
Mountains are impassable [Terrain|TERRAIN] too rugged to be of any economic or military use.
Hills are a minor [Terrain|TERRAIN] obstacle.
[*]Costs 1 additional [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Reduces the [Supply|SUPPLY] available to [Units|UNIT] by 1.
[*]Grants a 25% [Defense|DEFENSE] bonus.
[*]Blocks [Vision|VISION], unless...
Standing [COLOR:EMPHASIS]on[/COLOR] a Hill makes it possible to see over [Forests|FOREST] and other Hills, making them ideal Tiles for [EXPLORER:S] to visit while exploring.
Hills can also contain valuable [Minerals|MINERAL] like [DEPOSIT-IRON:S] and [DEPOSIT-STONE:S].
Grassland is the most hospitable type of [Terrain|TERRAIN] in AtG.
[*]Costs only 1 [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Provides 3 [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
These values are modified when a Grassland [Tile|TILE] also contains rough Terrain like a [Forest|FOREST] or [HILL], or is affected by [Cold|COLD] or [Weather|WEATHER].
Grasslands are also home to a variety of [Harvestable|HARVEST] [Plants|PLANT] and [Animals|ANIMAL], such as [DEPOSIT-WHEAT:S] and [DEPOSIT-SHEEP:S].
Steppes are a flat, dry type of [Terrain|TERRAIN].
[*]Costs only 1 [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Provides 2 [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
These values are modified when a Steppe [Tile|TILE] also contains rough Terrain like a [Forest|FOREST] or [HILL], or is affected by [Cold|COLD] or [Weather|WEATHER].
Steppes are home to a variety of [Harvestable|HARVEST] [Plants|PLANT] and [Animals|ANIMAL], such as [DEPOSIT-WHEAT:S] and [DEPOSIT-CATTLE:S].
Deserts are a flat, inhospitable type of [Terrain|TERRAIN].
[*]Costs only 1 [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Provides NO [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
The Movement Cost of a Desert [Tile|TILE] is modified when it also contains rough Terrain like a [HILL].
Deserts never contain [Harvestable|HARVEST] [Resources|RESOURCE] or any vegetation.
Marshes are a type of [Terrain|TERRAIN] that's incredibly difficult to enter but sometimes contains [Harvestable|HARVEST] [Mineral Deposits|MINERAL].
[*]Costs all remaining [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] PLUS those of the following [Turn|TURN] to enter.
[*]Provides 1 [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
Because of their harsh movement penalty Marshes are usually best avoided, except to [Harvest|HARVEST] [Minerals|MINERAL] like [IRON] or [COAL].
Tundra is a flat, inhospitable type of [Terrain|TERRAIN].
[*]Costs only 1 [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Provides 1 [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
These values are modified when a Tundra [Tile|TILE] also contains rough Terrain like a [Forest|FOREST] or [HILL], or is affected by [Cold|COLD] or [Weather|WEATHER].
Tundra parts of the map often contain a great deal of [Harvestable|HARVEST] [Minerals|MINERAL] and sometimes even [Animals|ANIMAL].
Forests are a major [Terrain|TERRAIN] obstacle which can be [Harvested|HARVEST] for [TIMBER].
[*]Lose [COLOR:EMPHASIS]all[/COLOR] remaining [Move Points|MOVE-POINT] after entering.
[*]Reduces the [Supply|SUPPLY] available to [Units|UNIT] by 1.
[*]Grants a 25% [Defense|DEFENSE] bonus.
[*]Blocks [Vision|VISION] (unless standing on a nearby [HILL]).
Forests are also the only source of [TIMBER], a [Resource|RESOURCE] needed to [Construct|CONSTRUCT] [Structures|STRUCTURE], and therefore vital for most of the game.
Forests should not be confused with the more easily-traversed but Timber-less [BRUSH-LEAFY].
Brush is a [Terrain|TERRAIN] obstacle which contains no useful [Resources|RESOURCE].
[*]Costs 1 additional [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Reduces the [Supply|SUPPLY] available to [Units|UNIT] by 1.
[*]Grants a 10% [Defense|DEFENSE] bonus.
[*]Require 1 additional [Vision|VISION] to see through (unless standing on a nearby [HILL]).
Brush should not be confused with [Forests|FOREST], which are much harder to move through but contain [Harvestable|HARVEST] [TIMBER].
Snow is a temporary [Season|SEASON]-based [Terrain|TERRAIN] type which replaces whatever was previously on a [Tile|TILE].
[*]Costs 2 [Move PointS|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Provides NO [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
Blizzards are a temporary [Weather|WEATHER]-based [Terrain|TERRAIN] type which can be added to a [Tile|TILE].
[*]Tiles with a Blizzard cannot be entered or left.
[*]Provides NO [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
[*]Greatly increases the [Vision|VISION] cost to see into and out of.
Scorched is a temporary [Weather|WEATHER]-based [Terrain|TERRAIN] type which replaces whatever was previously on a [Tile|TILE].
[*][Structures|STRUCTURE] on a Scorched Tile will shut down and go [Offline|OFFLINE].
[*]Provides NO [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
Rain is a temporary [Weather|WEATHER]-based [Terrain|TERRAIN] type which can be added to a [Tile|TILE].
[*]Costs 1 additional [Move Point|MOVE-POINT] to enter.
[*]Greatly increases the [Vision|VISION] cost to see into and out of.
Flooded is a temporary [Weather|WEATHER]-based [Terrain|TERRAIN] type which can be added to a [Tile|TILE] after a large amount of [RAIN].
[*]Flooded Tiles cannot be entered or left.
[*]Provides NO [Supply|SUPPLY] to [Units|UNIT].
[*]Greatly increases the [Vision|VISION] cost to see into and out of.
Ice is a temporary [Season|SEASON]-based water [Terrain|TERRAIN] type which can be added to a [Tile|TILE].
[*]Tiles with Ice cannot be entered or left.

Rivers are a supreme [Terrain|TERRAIN] obstacle which cannot be crossed (unless frozen in the winter).
Rivers should not be confused with the smaller, crossable [RIVER-SMALL:S].
Streams are a major [Terrain|TERRAIN] obstacle.
[*]Lose [COLOR:EMPHASIS]all[/COLOR] remaining [Move Points|MOVE-POINT] after entering.
Streams should not be confused with larger, uncrossable [RIVER-LARGE:S].
This [Clan|CLAN] is made up of unemployed peasants who eat [FOOD] but don't [Produce|PRODUCE] anything. [Train|TRAIN] them in a [Profession|PROFESSION] as soon as you can!

Hunters can [Harvest|HARVEST] [MEAT] by [Moving|MOVE-POINT] to any [Tile|TILE] containing an [Animal Herd|ANIMAL] and [Foraging|FORAGE]. They can also [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] [DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-ANIMAL:S].
Hunters are also the first [Warrior|WARRIOR] [Profession|PROFESSION] available to you, though this isn't their primary job and so they'll have trouble in most serious battles.
[Training|TRAIN] a [Clan|CLAN] as a Hunter can be a good way to keep your tribe fed at the start of the game.

The Archer is the first 'full-time' [Warrior|WARRIOR] [Profession|PROFESSION] available in At the Gates. They are useful in the early game but should be replaced with [SPEARMAN:S] if you expect heavy fighting.
You can increase an Archer's [Power|POWER] by [Upgrading|UPGRADE] them with [WEAPONS] and [ARMOR].
Feast Masters [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [FAME].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [ALCOHOL].

Watchmen can greatly extend your [Borders|BORDERS] and allow you to [Harvest|HARVEST] distant [Resource Deposits|DEPOSIT] by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] [WATCHTOWER:S] from [TIMBER]. This can be done even outside your existing Borders.
Huntsmen increase the amount of [MEAT] [Produced|PRODUCE] by your [HUNTER:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [WEAPONS].
Priests [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [KNOWLEDGE], helping you [Learn|LEARN] new [Professions|PROFESSION] faster.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [OIL] and [CLOTH].
Clerics [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [KNOWLEDGE], helping you [Learn|LEARN] new [Professions|PROFESSION] faster.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [FINE-CLOTH], [OIL], and that a [Clan|CLAN] has been [Ennobled|NOBLE].
Honor Guards [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [FAME].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [ARMOR] and [WEAPONS].
Masters of Ceremony [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [FAME].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [FINE-CLOTH], [OIL], and that a [Clan|CLAN] has been [Ennobled|NOBLE].
Guildmasters [Produce|PRODUCE] [TREASURE] based on how many [Levels|LEVEL] a [Clan|CLAN] has in [HONOR]. They can also be [Upgraded|UPGRADE] to Produce [FAME].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT].
Having a Quartermaster allows you to [Upgrade|UPGRADE] [Warriors|WARRIOR] with 50 [WEAPONS], increasing their [Power|POWER] by 5.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT] and that a [Clan|CLAN] has been [Ennobled|NOBLE].
Tariff Collectors significantly increase the amount of [TREASURE] [Produced|PRODUCE] by your [TRADING-POST-1:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT].

Gatherers can [Harvest|HARVEST] [FRUIT], [HONEY], [GRAPES], and [OLIVES] by [Moving|MOVE-POINT] to any [Tile|TILE] containing a [source|DEPOSIT] of one of them and [Foraging|FORAGE]. They can also [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] [DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-PLANT:S].

During [Cold|COLD] months Gatherers are only able to [Forage|FORAGE] from [DEPOSIT-BEEHIVE:S] and [DEPOSIT-BERRIES:S] - this can be a good time to use them to [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] nearby [DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-PLANT:S].
[Training|TRAIN] a [Clan|CLAN] as a Gatherer can be a good way to keep your tribe fed at the start of the game.

Reapers can [Harvest|HARVEST] [WHEAT], [BARLEY] and [FLAX] by [Moving|MOVE-POINT] to any [Tile|TILE] containing a [source|DEPOSIT] of one of these [Plants|PLANT] and [Foraging|FORAGE]. They can also [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] [DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-PLANT:S].
During [Cold|COLD] months Reapers are unable to Harvest anything - this can be a good time to use them to [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] nearby [DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-PLANT:S].
[Training|TRAIN] a [Clan|CLAN] as a Reaper can be a good way to keep your tribe fed at the start of the game.

Farmers can [Construct|CONSTRUCT] [Farms|FARM] out of [TIMBER] or [STONE-BLOCKS] on [Plants|PLANT], and [APIARY-1:S] on [DEPOSIT-BEEHIVE:S]. These [Structures|STRUCTURE] [Produce|PRODUCE] more than [Foraging|FORAGE] with a [GATHERER] or [REAPER].
Later in the game, Farmers can also be assigned to advanced [STONE-BLOCKS:SINGULAR]-based Farms as [Apprentices|APPRENTICE], each one doubling the Structure's [Production|PRODUCE].
Beekeepers increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [HONEY].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TIMBER].
Bread Makers increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [WHEAT] and [BARLEY].
[Training|TRAIN] them is free, but they consume [TIMBER] each turn.
Bakers greatly increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [WHEAT] and [BARLEY].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STONE-BLOCKS], and they consume [COAL] each turn.
Ale Makers [Produce|PRODUCE] [ALCOHOL] from [BARLEY] or [WHEAT].
Brewers [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [ALCOHOL] from [BARLEY] or [WHEAT].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Winemakers [Produce|PRODUCE] [ALCOHOL] from [GRAPES] or [HONEY].
Winemakers [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [ALCOHOL] from [GRAPES] or [HONEY].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Briners use [SALT] to turn [FOOD] into a much larger amount of [FOOD].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Preservers use [SALT] to turn [FOOD] into a massive amount of [FOOD].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS] and [BOARDS].
Oil Pressers [Produce|PRODUCE] [OIL] from [OLIVES] or [FLAX].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Oil Cultivars [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [OIL] from [OLIVES] or [FLAX].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Gardeners increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [FRUIT], [HONEY], [GRAPES], and [OLIVES].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Tillers increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of all [Farms|FARM].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Harvesters increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of [GATHERER:S] and [REAPER:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].

Trappers can [Harvest|HARVEST] [PARCHMENT] or [CLOTH] (as leather) from [Animal Herds|ANIMAL] by [Foraging|FORAGE]. However, doing so depletes the herd much faster than [Producing|PRODUCE] them with the help of a [Pasture|PASTURE] and a refining [Profession|PROFESSION] like the [PARCHMENT-MAKER].
Trappers can also [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] [DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-ANIMAL:S].
[Training|TRAIN] a [Clan|CLAN] as a Trapper can be a good way to make use of nearby Animal Herds and get your economy off the ground at the start of the game.

Ranchers can [Construct|CONSTRUCT] [Pastures|PASTURE] out of [TIMBER] or [STONE-BLOCKS] on [Animal Herds|ANIMAL], adding them to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] and allowing other [Clans|CLAN] to [Produce|PRODUCE] more advanced [Resources|RESOURCE] from them (e.g. [MEAT-CUTTER:S]).

Later in the game, Ranchers can also be assigned to advanced [STONE-BLOCKS:SINGULAR]-based Pastures as [Apprentices|APPRENTICE], each one doubling the number of Animals added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE].
The Fisherman is a water-based [Profession|PROFESSION] which can [Harvest|HARVEST] large amounts of [FISH] from [DEPOSIT-FISH:S] by [Foraging|FORAGE].
They can only be [Trained|TRAIN] when your [SETTLEMENT] is next to a large body of water.
If you spot one or two [sources|DEPOSIT] of [FISH] nearby consider training a Fisherman, as this can be a great way to feed your tribe in the early game.
Trawlers double the amount of [FISH] [Produced|PRODUCE] by your [FISHERMAN:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [CLOTH].
Wool Collectors [Produce|PRODUCE] [WOOL], the amount being based on the number of [SHEEP] in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE]. Your Sheep are not harmed in the process!
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Shearers [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [WOOL], the amount being based on the number of [SHEEP] in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE]. Your Sheep are not harmed in the process!
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Ploughers increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of your [Farms|FARM].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires a small number of [HORSES].
Sledge Drivers increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [TIMBER].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires a small number of [HORSES].
Curers use [SALT] to turn [MEAT] into a larger amount of [FOOD].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Meat Cutters [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [MEAT] from [Animals|ANIMAL] added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] a [Pasture|PASTURE] on them.
Butchers [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [MEAT] from [Animals|ANIMAL] added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] a [Pasture|PASTURE] on them.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].

Parchment Makers [Produce|PRODUCE] [PARCHMENT] from [Animals|ANIMAL] added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] a [Pasture|PASTURE] on them. They are much more efficient in doing so than [TRAPPER:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Vellum Makers [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [PARCHMENT] from [Animals|ANIMAL] in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Horse Trainers [Produce|PRODUCE] [WARHORSES] from [HORSES] added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] a [Pasture|PASTURE] on them.
Meat Smokers dramatically increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [MEAT].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STONE-BLOCKS], and they consume [TIMBER] each turn.

Cheesemakers [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [CHEESE], the quantity being based on the number of [CATTLE] and [SHEEP] added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] a [Pasture|PASTURE] on them.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [BOARDS], and they consume [SALT] each turn.
Fishmongers dramatically increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [FISH].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [CLOTH].
Shepherds allow your [Animal|ANIMAL] [Pastures|PASTURE] to [Produce|PRODUCE] new Livestock.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].

Diggers can [Harvest|HARVEST] [Mineral|MINERAL] [Resources|RESOURCE] by [Moving|MOVE-POINT] to any [Tile|TILE] containing a Mineral Deposit and [Foraging|FORAGE]. They can also [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] [DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-MINERAL:S].
[Training|TRAIN] a [Clan|CLAN] as a Digger can be a good way to make use of nearby Minerals and get your economy off the ground at the start of the game.
Miners can [Construct|CONSTRUCT] [Mines|MINE] out of [TIMBER] or [STONE-BLOCKS] on [Minerals|MINERAL]. Mines [Produce|PRODUCE] more than [Foraging|FORAGE] with a [DIGGER].
Later in the game, Miners can also be assigned to advanced [STONE-BLOCKS:SINGULAR]-based Mines as [Apprentices|APPRENTICE], each one doubling the Structure's [Production|PRODUCE].
Dredgers increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of your [DIGGER:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Blacksmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] [TOOLS] from [IRON].
[Harvesting|HARVEST] [IRON] with a [DIGGER] and then having a Blacksmith make Tools from it can be a good way to get your economy off the ground in the early game.
Weaponsmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] [WEAPONS] from [IRON].
[Harvesting|HARVEST] [IRON] with a [DIGGER] and then having a Weaponsmith make Weapons from it can be a good way to get your economy off the ground in the early game.
Bladesmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [WEAPONS] from [IRON].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Smelters massively increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [IRON].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STONE-BLOCKS], and they consume [COAL] each turn.
Anvil Makers triple your [Production|PRODUCE] of [TOOLS] and [WEAPONS].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS], and they consume [COAL] each turn.
Toolsmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] a large number of [TOOLS] from [IRON].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL].
Coal Makers create [COAL] from [TIMBER].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STONE-BLOCKS].
ERROR
Armorsmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] [ARMOR] from [IRON].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
ERROR
Steelmakers [Produce|PRODUCE] [STEEL] from [IRON] and [COAL].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Steel Toolsmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] [STEEL-TOOLS] from [STEEL].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Steel Weaponsmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] [STEEL-WEAPONS] from [STEEL].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Steel Armorsmiths [Produce|PRODUCE] [STEEL-ARMOR] from [STEEL].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Glassblowers increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [ALCOHOL] and non-[MEAT] types of [FOOD].
They consume [COAL] each turn.
Glassblowers dramatically increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [ALCOHOL] and non-[MEAT] types of [FOOD].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS], and they consume [COAL] each turn.
Coinmakers produce a massive amount of [TREASURE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS], and they consume [COAL] each turn.
Wood Collectors can [Harvest|HARVEST] [TIMBER] by [Moving|MOVE-POINT] to any [Tile|TILE] containing a [Forest|FOREST] (but not [BRUSH-LEAFY], which is similar) and [Foraging|FORAGE].
[Training|TRAIN] a [Clan|CLAN] as a Wood Collector can be a good way to make use of nearby Forests and get your economy off the ground at the start of the game.
Loggers can [Construct|CONSTRUCT] [LOGGING-CAMP-1:S], which [Produce|PRODUCE] [TIMBER] from [Tiles|TILE] containing a [Forest|FOREST] both under AND adjacent to the Structure.
Loggers can also be assigned as [Apprentices|APPRENTICE] to the advanced [LOGGING-CAMP-2] Constructed from [STONE-BLOCKS], each one increasing [TIMBER] Production by 4.
Potters increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of your [HUNTER:S], [GATHERER:S], and [REAPER:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TIMBER].
Wood Bundlers increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of your [WOOD-COLLECTOR:S].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Brickmakers [Produce|PRODUCE] [STONE-BLOCKS] from [COAL].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Kiln Masters [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [STONE-BLOCKS] from [COAL].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Weavers [Produce|PRODUCE] [CLOTH] from [WOOL] or [FLAX].
Coopers greatly increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [ALCOHOL] and non-[MEAT] types of [FOOD].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS], and they consume [BOARDS] each turn.
Woodcarvers [Produce|PRODUCE] [FAME] from [TIMBER].
Luthiers [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [FAME] from [BOARDS].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Woodsplitters [Produce|PRODUCE] [BOARDS] from [TIMBER].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Hewers [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [BOARDS] from [TIMBER].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Master Hewers [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [BOARDS] from [TIMBER].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Block Cutters [Produce|PRODUCE] [STONE-BLOCKS] from [STONE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Master Block Cutters [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [STONE-BLOCKS] from [STONE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Dyers [Produce|PRODUCE] [FINE-CLOTH] from [CLOTH].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS] and [BOARDS].
Tailors [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [CLOTH] from [WOOL] or [FLAX].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Couturiers [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [FINE-CLOTH] from [CLOTH].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS] and that a [Clan|CLAN] has been [Ennobled|NOBLE].
Stonecarvers [Produce|PRODUCE] [FAME] from [STONE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Sculptors [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [FAME] from [STONE-BLOCKS].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS] and that a [Clan|CLAN] has been [Ennobled|NOBLE].
Engineers increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of all [Mines|MINE].
Architects increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of all advanced [Structures|STRUCTURE] built from [STONE-BLOCKS].

Explorers excel at discovering any opportunities or threats that are nearby. They can cross rough [Terrain|TERRAIN] like [HILL:S] and [Forests|FOREST] easily, and are the only [Profession|PROFESSION] which can explore [Deserted Locations|DESERTED-LOCATION].
Explorers are also extremely fragile and can neither [Attack|ATTACK] enemies nor capture [Structures|STRUCTURE].
[Training|TRAIN] a [Clan|CLAN] as an Explorer on the first turn is often a good move, as this will give you more information with which to build strategies around.
Surveyors can [Identify|UNIDENTIFIED] ANY kind of [source of Resources|DEPOSIT] ([DEPOSIT-UNKNOWN-PLANT:S].
Like [EXPLORER:S] they ignore the [Movement|MOVE-POINT] penalty associated with rough [Terrain|TERRAIN].
Surveyors can can also create [PATH:S] on [Map Tiles|TILE] to greatly speed [Movement|MOVE-POINT] through it, which can be especially helpful in [heavily-Forested|FOREST] areas.

Traders can [Produce|PRODUCE] a huge amount of [TREASURE] by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] [TRADING-POST-1:S] out of [TIMBER] or [STONE-BLOCKS] on junctions where [Small Rivers|RIVER] meet one another or a body of water.
Additional Traders can also be assigned to [TRADING-POST-2:S] as [Apprentices|APPRENTICE], each one greatly increasing the amount of Treasure Produced.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TIMBER].
Guides increase the [Production|PRODUCE] of all [Foragers|FORAGE] (e.g. [HUNTER], [GATHERER], and [DIGGER]).
Bards [Produce|PRODUCE] [FAME].
Minstrels [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [FAME].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [BOARDS] and [ALCOHOL].
Lorekeepers [Produce|PRODUCE] [KNOWLEDGE].
Scribes [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [KNOWLEDGE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT].
Scholars [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [KNOWLEDGE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT].
Tinkerers [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [KNOWLEDGE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TOOLS].
Consuls [Produce|PRODUCE] a large amount of [PRESTIGE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT].

When you have an Instructor you can remove [Traits|CLAN-TRAIT] or [Crimes|CRIME] from [Clans|CLAN] in the [SETTLEMENT] by spending 50 [PARCHMENT] (right-click on the Clan Card icon to perform this action).
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT].
TODO"
Patrons [Produce|PRODUCE] an enormous amount of [FAME].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [TREASURE].
Inventors [Produce|PRODUCE] a massive amount of [KNOWLEDGE].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-TOOLS].
Merchants greatly increase your [Production|PRODUCE] of [CLOTH], [ALCOHOL], and [OIL].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [PARCHMENT].
Catapulters are highly specialized [Units|UNIT] which excel in weakening [Dug In|DIG-IN] enemies [Armies|ARMY] by reducing their [Supply|SUPPLY]. However, they are slow, vulnerable and expensive.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [BOARDS] and [STEEL].

Galleys are both the battleships and transports of late antiquity. They can ferry 1 [Unit|UNIT] or your [SETTLEMENT] over the water at high speed, which can very useful when claiming [Deposits|DEPOSIT] on nearby islands, migrating, invading distant neighbors, or simply moving land-based [Clans|CLAN] back and forth.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [CLOTH] and [TIMBER].

Supply Ships provide [Supply|SUPPLY] to adjacent land [Tiles|TILE], making them a vital element of any major naval operation. Unlike the Supply Camp, Supply Ships do not need to be within range of an existing [Supply|SUPPLY] source. It is impossible to [Besiege|SIEGE] Armies adjacent to a friendly Supply Ship.

Spearmen are the front-line [Warriors|WARRIOR] of At the Gates. Although feared by [Bandits|BANDIT] they are outclassed by [Cavalry|CAVALRY] on flat ground, though more capable in rough [Terrain|TERRAIN] and much cheaper.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [WEAPONS].

Men-At-Arms are late antiquity's most powerful front-line combatants. Feared by all other types of [Infantry|INFANTRY], they are outclassed only by [HEAVY-CAVALRY:S] and [ROMAN-HEAVY-CAVALRY:S] in open [Terrain|TERRAIN].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-WEAPONS] and [ARMOR].

Lancers are fast, but expensive to [Train|TRAIN] and less effective in rough [Terrain|TERRAIN] due to their [Supply|SUPPLY] cost. They are ideal for recon, raiding, cutting off enemy [Supply|SUPPLY], and dealing with sudden emergencies.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [WARHORSES] and [WEAPONS].

This early form of Knights began to see use as the Roman Empire still clung to life. They are unmatched on the battlefield, but extremely expensive to [Train|TRAIN] and less effective in rough [Terrain|TERRAIN] due to their [Supply|SUPPLY] cost. The bulky armor they wear makes them not much faster than [Infantry|INFANTRY] but woe to any foe which dares stand and fights!
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-WEAPONS], [STEEL-ARMOR], [WARHORSES], and that a [Clan|CLAN] has been [Ennobled|NOBLE].

Horse Archers are elusive and deadly. They are much faster than Heavy Cavalry, and tougher to pin down than other types of Light Cavalry. The Horse Archer's only weakness is poor performance in rough [Terrain|TERRAIN]. Only the nomadic Huns and Avars can [Train|TRAIN] them.
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [WARHORSES] and [WEAPONS].
Legions are still formidable foes, but no longer the invincible force of centuries past. They are stronger than basic [Infantry|INFANTRY], but cost much more to [Train|TRAIN].
[Training|TRAIN] them requires [STEEL-WEAPONS] and [STEEL-ARMOR].

Equites are fast, but expensive to [Train|TRAIN] and less effective in rough [Terrain|TERRAIN]. They are ideal for recon, raiding, cutting off enemy [Supply|SUPPLY], and dealing with sudden emergencies.

Cataphracts are unmatched on the battlefield, but extremely expensive to [Train|TRAIN] and less effective in rough [Terrain|TERRAIN]. The bulky armor they wear makes them no faster than [Infantry|INFANTRY], but woe to any foe which stands and fights!
[Unmounted|INFANTRY] [Bandits|BANDIT] are are easily driven off due to their low [Morale|MORALE], but can still pose a threat to undefended [Structures|STRUCTURE] and [Civilians|CIVILIAN].

Bandit Raiders are weaker than the [LIGHT-CAVALRY:S] you can [Train|TRAINING], but still fast and dangerous. They are especially a threat to undefended [Structures|STRUCTURE], which they can [Pillage|PILLAGE] more quickly than [Infantry|INFANTRY].
Slaves are weaker than any other type of [Warrior|WARRIOR] [Unit|UNIT], but still pose a threat to undefended [Civilians|CIVILIAN]. They have high [Morale|MORALE] but very little [Power|POWER].

column2: TEXT.Description.Road.Roman","Roman Roads are engineering marvels of antiquity, and allow [Units|UNIT] to [Move|MOVE-POINT] over them very quickly."

column3: [SURVEYOR:S] can [Construct|CONSTRUCT] Paths in order to negate the [Movement|MOVE-POINT] penalty associated with rough [Terrain|TERRAIN] like [HILL:S] and [Forests|FOREST].

Note that for an [Army|ARMY] to receive this Movement bonus both the [Tile|TILE] it's moving from [COLOR:EMPHASIS]and[/COLOR] the one being entered must contain a Path - simply moving onto or off of one has no effect.
Score is a general indicator of how well your game is going.
[*]-2 for every [Turn|TURN] your tribe is [Starving|STARVE]
[*]+3 per [Clan|CLAN]
[*]+2 per [Structure|STRUCTURE] built, captured, or [Pillaged|PILLAGE]
[*]+2 per [Roman|ROME] Clan killed
[*]+2 per ship sunk
[*]+1 per non-Roman Clan killed
[*]+2 per [Bandit|BANDIT] Camp destroyed
[*]+3 per Bandit Fort/Slave Camp destroyed
As your Fame grows new [Clans|CLAN] hear of your deeds and join your tribe.
With each Clan that joins fewer remain who are interested in your leadership, and the rate new Clans arrive will slow down considerably.

You start the game [Producing|PRODUCE] 4 Fame per [Turn|TURN], but this number can be increased dramatically by [Training|TRAIN] Clans in [Professions|PROFESSION] that [Produce|PRODUCE] Fame, like the [BARD], [FEAST-MASTER], and [WOODCARVER]. [Declaring a Kingdom|DECLARE-KINGDOM] provides a massive boost to Fame, but fixes your [SETTLEMENT] in place, and is an option that should be avoided during the first few years of the game.
As your Prestige grows you will acquire diplomatic [Emissaries|EMISSARY].
You automatically produce a small amount of Prestige each turn (unless you play as Drest of the Picts). You can acquire more by [Training|TRAIN] Clans in the [CONSUL] [Profession|PROFESSION].
The more Knowledge you [Produce|PRODUCE] the faster you can [Learn|LEARN] new [Professions|PROFESSION] to [Train|TRAIN] your [Clans|CLAN] in and [Upgrades|UPGRADE] to make them even more powerful.

You start the game [Producing|PRODUCE] 4 Knowledge per [Turn|TURN], but this number can be increased dramatically by Training Clans in Knowledge-Producing Professions like [LOREKEEPER], [PRIEST], and [TINKERER].
Treasure allows you to purchase other [Resources|RESOURCE] from [Caravans|CARAVAN].

The three primary ways to acquire Treasure are selling Resources you don't need to Caravans, [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] [TRADING-POST-1:S], and Constructing [Mines|MINE] on rare [Mineral Deposits|MINERAL] like Gold or Gems. You also receive a small amount of Treasure for [Pillaging|PILLAGE] enemy [Structures|STRUCTURE].

Food is the game's most important [Resource|RESOURCE] as if you run out your Tribe will [Starve|STARVE]. Unlike all other Resources it is [stored as a number of Turns|STORED-TURN], rather than a specific quantity.
Each of your [Clans'|CLAN] [Families|FAMILY] [Consumes|CONSUME] 0.2 Food per [Turn|TURN]. You start a game with 3 Clans of 1 Family Consuming a total of 0.6 Food per Turn.

Much of your early Food will come from [Foraging|FORAGE], often with a [GATHERER]. Later, [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] [Farms|STRUCTURE] and [Pastures|PASTURE] provides a means to [Produce|PRODUCE] much more.
Wheat is a type of [FOOD] that can be [Harvested|HARVEST] from special [sources|DEPOSIT] by [REAPER:S] and [WHEAT-FARM-1:S].
Wheat can also be converted into [ALCOHOL] by an [ALE-MAKER].

column2: TEXT.Description.Resource.Fish","Fish are a type of [FOOD] that can be [Harvested|HARVEST] from special [sources|DEPOSIT] by [FISHERMAN:S]."

column1: TEXT.Description.Resource.Meat,"Meat is a type of [FOOD] that can be [Produced|PRODUCE] by [MEAT-CUTTER:S] from [Animals|ANIMAL] in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE]."

TEXT.Description.Resource.Cheese,"Cheese is a type of [FOOD] that can be [Produced|PRODUCE] by [CHEESEMAKER:S], the amount based on how many [CATTLE] and [SHEEP] you have in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE]."
TEXT.Description.Resource.Fruit,"Fruit is a type of [FOOD] that can be [Harvested|HARVEST] from special [sources|DEPOSIT] by [REAPER:S] and [WHEAT-FARM-1:S]."

column3: Warhorses are needed to [Train|TRAIN] fast and powerful [Cavalry|CAVALRY].
1 [WARHORSES:SINGULAR] is automatically turned into 5 [FOOD] if the Tribe is starving.
Horses can be used in the same way as other [Animals|ANIMAL], and also turned into [WARHORSES] by a [HORSE-TRAINER].
1 [HORSES:SINGULAR] from your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] is automatically turned into 5 [FOOD] if the Tribe is starving.
Sheep can be used in the same way as other [Animals|ANIMAL], and also used to create [CLOTH] with the help of a [WOOL-COLLECTOR] and [WEAVER].
1 [SHEEP:SINGULAR] from your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] is automatically turned into 4 [FOOD] if the Tribe is starving.
Cattle can be used in the same way as other [Animals|ANIMAL].
1 [CATTLE:SINGULAR] from your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] is automatically turned into 5 [FOOD] if the Tribe is starving.
Pigs can be used in the same way as other [Animals|ANIMAL].
1 [PIGS:SINGULAR] from your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] is automatically turned into 4 [FOOD] if the Tribe is starving.
Deer can be eaten as [FOOD] with the help of a [HUNTER], or turned into [PARCHMENT] or [CLOTH] (Leather) by a [TRAPPER].
Unlike other [Animals|ANIMAL] Deer can only be [Foraged|FORAGE] and not added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] via a [Pasture|PASTURE], making them not quite as useful.
Steel Armor can be used to [Train|TRAIN] advanced [Infantry|INFANTRY] and [Cavalry|CAVALRY], and are required by some advanced [Upgrades|UPGRADE].
Steel Armor is forged from [STEEL] by a [STEEL-ARMORSMITH]."
Steel Weapons are needed to [Train|TRAIN] all advanced [Warrior|WARRIOR] [Professions|PROFESSION], and are required by some advanced [Upgrades|UPGRADE].
Steel Weapons are forged from [STEEL] by a [STEEL-WEAPONSMITH]."
Steel Tools are needed to [Train|TRAIN] many advanced [Civilian|CIVILIAN] [Professions|PROFESSION], and are required by many advanced [Upgrades|UPGRADE].
Steel Tools are forged from [STEEL] by a [STEELMAKER]."
Steel can be forged into [STEEL-TOOLS], [STEEL-WEAPONS], and [STEEL-ARMOR] by advanced [METALWORKING] [Professions|PROFESSION].
Steel is created from [IRON] and [COAL] by a [STEELMAKER]."
Armor can be used to [Train|TRAIN] advanced [Infantry|INFANTRY] and [Cavalry|CAVALRY], and is required by some advanced [Upgrades|UPGRADE].
Armor is forged from [IRON] by an [ARMORSMITH]."
Weapons are needed to [Train|TRAIN] nearly all [Warrior|WARRIOR] [Professions|PROFESSION], and can be used to [Upgrade|UPGRADE] their [Power|POWER] even further.
Weapons are forged from [IRON] by a [WEAPONSMITH]."
Tools are needed to [Train|TRAIN] many [Settled|SETTLED] [Civilian|CIVILIAN] [Professions|PROFESSION], and are required by many early game [Upgrades|UPGRADE].
Tools are forged from [IRON] by a [BLACKSMITH]."
Iron is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [Mineral Deposits|MINERAL] by [DIGGER:S] or [IRON-MINE-1:S].
Iron can be forged into [TOOLS] by a [BLACKSMITH], into [WEAPONS] by a [WEAPONSMITH], or into [STEEL] by a [STEELMAKER]."
Coal is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [Mineral Deposits|MINERAL] by [DIGGER:S] or [IRON-MINE-1:S].
[STEELMAKER:S] can combine Coal with [IRON] to make [STEEL], and Coal is required by a handful of advanced [FOOD]-boosting [Professions|PROFESSION] like the [BAKER]."
Stone Blocks are created from [STONE] by a [BLOCK-CUTTER].

Stock Blocks are a very important [Resource|RESOURCE], as they can be used to [Construct|CONSTRUCT] permanent [Structures|STRUCTURE] which never [Degrade|DEGRADE]. They're also required by some [Profession|PROFESSION] [Upgrades|UPGRADE]."
Stone is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [Mineral Deposits|MINERAL] by [DIGGER:S] or [STONE-QUARRY-1:S].

The main use of Stone is converting it into [STONE-BLOCKS] with a [BLOCK-CUTTER] and using those to [Construct|CONSTRUCT] [Structures|STRUCTURE] which never [Degrade|DEGRADE]. It can also be used in other miscellaneous ways, such as having a [STONECARVER] turn it into [FAME]."
Boards are created from [TIMBER] by [WOODSPLITTER:S], and can be used in a few ways:
[*]Spent to add [Apprentice Slots|APPRENTICE] to advanced [STONE-BLOCKS:SINGULAR]-based [Structures|STRUCTURE].
[*]Spent to acquire some [Profession|PROFESSION] [Upgrades|UPGRADE].
[*]Used to build [GALLEY:S] and [CATAPULT:S] in the late game."
Timber is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [Forests|FOREST] by [WOOD-COLLECTOR:S] or [LOGGING-CAMP-1:S], and can be used in a variety of ways:
[*]Traded to the [Caravan|CARAVAN] in exchange for other [Resources|RESOURCE].
[*]Used for economically-useful purposes (such as having a [WOODCARVER] turn it into [FAME].
[*]Converted into [BOARDS] by a [WOODSPLITTER].
Parchment is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [Animals|ANIMAL] by [TRAPPER:S] or [Produced|PRODUCE] from [Stockpiled|STOCKPILE] Animals by [PARCHMENT-MAKER:S], and can be used in a variety of ways:
[*][Training|TRAIN] Clans in some [Civilian|CIVILIAN] [Professions|PROFESSION] (especially those in [DISCOVERY]).
[*]Spent to acquire some [Profession|PROFESSION] [Upgrades|UPGRADE].
[*]Used to [Declare Your Kingdom|DECLARE-KINGDOM].
[*]Spent to remove [Traits|CLAN-TRAIT] or [Crimes|CRIME] from a Clan (via the [INSTRUCTOR]).

Fine Cloth is an advanced [Resource|RESOURCE] mainly can be used to [Ennoble|NOBLE] [Clans|CLAN], but is also used to [Train|TRAIN] them in a couple late-game [Civilian|CIVILIAN] [Professions|PROFESSION] (e.g. [CLERIC]).
Fine Cloth is created from normal [CLOTH] by a [COUTURIER].
Cloth is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [Animals|ANIMAL] by [TRAPPER:S] (as Leather) or [Produced|PRODUCE] from [WOOL] or [FLAX] by [WEAVER:S], and can be used in a variety of ways:
[*]Spent in the Clans Screen to increase your [Clan Limit|POPCAP].
[*]Spent to acquire some [Profession|PROFESSION] [Upgrades|UPGRADE] (e.g. for [FISHERMAN:S] or [GATHERER:S]).
[*]Spent to [Train|TRAIN] Clans in some [Professions|PROFESSION] (e.g. [PRIEST], [GALLEY]).
[*]Spent to [Heal|DAMAGE] wounded [Warriors|WARRIOR] (along with [OIL]).
Wool is [Produced|PRODUCE] by [WOOL-COLLECTOR:S], the amount being based on how many [SHEEP] you have in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE]. Your Sheep are not harmed in the process!
[WEAVER:S] can turn [WOOL] into [CLOTH].
Flax is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [DEPOSIT-FLAX:S] by [REAPER:S] or [FLAX-FARM-1:S], and can be used in a few ways:
[*]Traded to the [Caravan|CARAVAN] in exchange for other [Resources|RESOURCE].
[*]Converted into [CLOTH] by a [WEAVER].
[*]Converted into [OIL] by an [OIL-PRESSER].
Oil is [Produced|PRODUCE] from [OLIVES] or [FLAX], and can be used in a few ways:
[*]Traded to the [Caravan|CARAVAN] in exchange for other [Resources|RESOURCE].
[*]Spent to [Train|TRAIN] Clans in some advanced [Professions|PROFESSION], especially in [HONOR].
[*]Spent to [Heal|DAMAGE] wounded [Warriors|WARRIOR] (along with [CLOTH]).
Olives are automatically converted into [FOOD] when your tribe has nothing else to eat.
A better use though is converting Olives into [OIL] with an [OIL-PRESSER].
Alcohol is [Produced|PRODUCE] from [GRAPES], [HONEY], [BARLEY], and [WHEAT]. It can be used in a few ways:
[*]Traded to the [Caravan|CARAVAN] in exchange for other [Resources|RESOURCE].
[*]Spent to temporarily increase the [Mood|MOOD] of a Clan (button appears on the Clan Card).
[*]Spent to [Train|TRAIN] Clans in a couple [Professions|PROFESSION] (such as [FEAST-MASTER]).
Grapes are automatically converted into [FOOD] when your tribe has nothing else to eat.
A better use though is converting Grapes into [ALCOHOL] with a [WINEMAKER].
Honey is automatically converted into [FOOD] when your tribe has nothing else to eat.
A better use though is converting Honey into [ALCOHOL] with a [WINEMAKER].
Barley is automatically converted into [FOOD] when your tribe has nothing else to eat.
A better use though is converting Barley into [ALCOHOL] with an [ALE-MAKER].
Salt is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [DEPOSIT-SALT:S] by [DIGGER:S] or [SALT-MINE-1:S], and can be used in a couple ways:
[*]Traded to the [Caravan|CARAVAN] in exchange for other [Resources|RESOURCE].
[*]Used by [Professions|PROFESSION] like the [BRINER] and [CHEESEMAKER] to greatly increase your [FOOD] [Production|PRODUCE].

column1: TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Huns,"Can Train Horse Archers, and starts the game with one. Cannot own Structures."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Vandals,"Receives gifts of Food or Treasure from Rome every couple years. Locked in a permanent Alliance with all Roman Factions."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Alemanni,"All Clans will have at least one positive Trait and no fully negative Traits. -50% Fame."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Picts,"Starts the game with 30 Food and 8 Clans (default is 3)."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Lombards,"Starts the game with 40 Food and all Resource Deposits Identified. Unable to Train any Clans in Foraging Professions."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Franks,"+1 Mood bonus for all Clans. Permanently at war with all Factions which don't follow the 'Nicene Christianity' Religion."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Slavs,"Units no longer require Supply except when inside another Faction's Borders."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Saxons,"Starts the game next to the ocean with a free Galley. Bonuses provided by Deserted Locations doubled."
TEXT.Faction.FactionTraitDescription.Avars,"+1 Move Point for all Units. Can Train Horse Archers. All Units require 1 additional Supply."
TEXT.Description.DevLevel.VeryPrimitive,"Very Primitive [Factions|FACTION] start basically at the level of the Human Player: a single [SETTLEMENT] and a handful of [Clans|CLAN]."
TEXT.Description.DevLevel.Primitive,"Primitive [Factions|FACTION] start with somewhat more than the Human Player, and are roughly equivalent in to where the player is 50 turns into the game."
TEXT.Description.DevLevel.Developed,"Developed [Factions|FACTION] are in what would roughly be a mid-game situation for the Human Player."
TEXT.Description.DevLevel.Advanced,"Advanced [Factions|FACTION] are powerful, as much so as the Human Player can possibly get by the late-game."
TEXT.Description.DevLevel.VeryAdvanced,"Very Advanced [Factions|FACTION] are juggernauts the Human Player can never hope to compete with."
TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Minor,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Minor' Trait are passive players who don't compete for victory."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Roman,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Roman' Trait are civilized and typically highly-advanced, and generally look and act very differently from Factions which don't have the Trait."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Horsemen,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Horsemen' Trait are focused on mounted warfare and a nomadic lifestyle, and generally look and act very differently from Factions which don't have the Trait."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Maritime,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Maritime' Trait are focused on ships and a sea-based lifestyle, and generally look and act very differently from Factions which don't have the Trait."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Knowledge.LargePenalty,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Knowledge - Large Penalty' Trait are [COLOR:EMPHASIS]much[/COLOR] weaker in terms of [KNOWLEDGE] than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Knowledge.Penalty,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Knowledge - Penalty' Trait are weaker in terms of [KNOWLEDGE] than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Knowledge.Bonus,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Knowledge - Bonus' Trait are stronger in terms of [KNOWLEDGE] than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Knowledge.LargeBonus,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Knowledge - Large Bonus' Trait are [COLOR:EMPHASIS]much[/COLOR] stronger in terms of [KNOWLEDGE] than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Economy.LargePenalty,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Economy - Large Penalty' Trait are [COLOR:EMPHASIS]much[/COLOR] weaker in terms of Economy than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Economy.Penalty,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Economy - Penalty' Trait are weaker in terms of Economy than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Economy.Bonus,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Economy - Bonus' Trait are stronger in terms of Economy than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Outposts.LargePenalty,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Outposts - Large Penalty' Trait are [COLOR:EMPHASIS]much[/COLOR] weaker in terms of defensive structures than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.FactionTrait.Stockpile.LargePenalty,"[Factions|FACTION] with the 'Resource Stockpile - Large Penalty' Trait are [COLOR:EMPHASIS]much[/COLOR] weaker in terms of their stockpile of [FOOD] and other [Resources|RESOURCE] than their [Development Level|DEVELOPMENT-LEVEL] would normally suggest."

TEXT.Description.Mood.Resentful,"When Resentful a [Clan|CLAN] stops working entirely, and also reduces the [Morale|MORALE] of other Clans on the tile. This state is permanent."
TEXT.Description.Mood.Angry,"When Angry a [Clan|CLAN] becomes much less effective, both economically and militarily."
TEXT.Description.Mood.Upset,"When Upset a [Clan|CLAN] becomes slightly less effective, both economically and militarily."
TEXT.Description.Mood.Content,"[Clans|CLAN] are Content by default, unless something changes their [Mood|MOOD]."
TEXT.Description.Mood.Happy,"When Happy a [Clan|CLAN] becomes slightly more effective, both economically and militarily."
TEXT.Description.Mood.Jubilant,"When Jubilant a [Clan|CLAN] becomes much more effective, both economically and militarily."

column3: Declaring your tribe as a Kingdom permanently fixes your [SETTLEMENT] in place, but offers some significant bonuses in return.

Because you'll no longer be able to [move|MOVE-POINT] your Settlement after Declaring a Kingdom make sure you're in a good place you can continue to expand from before doing so! As a general rule of thumb you won't want to perform this action during the first few years of the game.
You can spend [ALCOHOL] to temporarily improve the [Mood|MOOD] of a [Clan|CLAN].
The price of doing so doubles each time a Clan's Mood is improved, which may be done up to a maximum of 5 times.
Performing this action will cost [###:COST] [???:RESOURCE|###:COST] and increase this Clan's Mood from [???:CURRENT-MOOD] to [???:NEW-MOOD] for [###:NUM-TURNS] [Turn|Turns|###:NUM-TURNS].
Once you have sufficient [Resources|RESOURCE] you may send [Clans|CLAN] to Rome to be [Trained|TRAIN] as [ROMAN-LEGION:S].

Performing this action results in the Clan you choose disappearing from the map for 24 [Turns|TURN], after which it reappears in your Settlement in its new role. Once you have 5 Legions you may use them to claim the title of 'Magister Militum', which is one way of winning the game.
Becoming 'Magister Militum' is one way of winning the game (the other being to capture a Roman capital).

Doing so requires you to send 5 [Clans|CLAN] to Rome to be [Trained|TRAIN] as [ROMAN-LEGION:S]. Once this force has been assembled in your Settlement you may send them forth, at which point they disappear from the map and one year (24 [Turns|TURN]) later they will arrive, seizing military and political control of Rome and making you the most powerful leader in Europe.
